# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Middleware for Next.js that implements authentication. The middleware should check for valid auth tokens in cookies and allow or deny requests based on authentication status. Handle both authenticated and unauthenticated cases appropriately.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// This is the type of request your handler will receive
type Request = {
  headers?: Record<string, string | string[]>;
};
  
export default function (req: Request, res: any) { // We don't know what 'res' looks like at this point. You might need to import the `Response` type if you want a dynamic response size or other settings. 
    const token = req?.headers['authorization'];  
    
	if (token === process.env.API_TOKEN) { // Authenticated request... we are allowed in.... go ahead! 🎉🚀️✨☮‍♂️ ᐅº æ É É§Ç©€
		return res.status(200).json({ message: 'Authenticated successfully.' });   // Your next handler or middleware can now run here as well, if it exists in your Next.js app! 🚀💨 ☄️‍♂️
	} else {	                                                                   
        return res                     .status(401).json({ message: 'Unauthorized.' }); // The client was not authenticated, send a `403 Forbidden` status code and an optional error if they attempted to access something they do not have permission for! ⚠‍🔴
	}   	                                                                      }   else {     return res.status(200).json({ message: 'Unauthenticated request.' }); // A Non-Authed client made a req... go ahead and send them back to the original site or an error if they were trying not to be authenticate, then proceed as usual! 🚀💨 ☄️‍♂️    }
}`;   // If you are making requests from other hosts for testing purposes (not on Vercel), make sure `res.status(200).json({message: 'Unauthenticated request.'}) never runs, otherwise it will stop the process! ☄️‍♂
